summary: Implemented browser push notifications for LEAMSS Portal. Users can now receive desktop alerts even when the portal is closed. Feature includes: service worker, VAPID key authentication, subscription management, and integration with existing notification system.

new_features_implemented.description: Desktop alerts using Web Push API and service workers
components: /app/frontend/public/sw.js - Service worker for handling push events
/app/frontend/src/hooks/usePushNotifications.js - React hook for push subscription management

endpoints: GET /api/push/vapid-public-key - Returns VAPID public key for subscription

new_features_implemented.description: Secure push notifications using VAPID (Voluntary Application Server Identification)

test_results.vapid_public_key_endpoint: PASSED - Returns valid VAPID public key
test_results.service_worker: PASSED - Registered and ready for push events

critical_code_review_comments: Push notifications require HTTPS in production - this is already satisfied by the preview URL
Service worker is registered at root scope (/) to receive all push events
Invalid/expired subscriptions are automatically cleaned up on delivery failure
Push notification button only shows when browser supports it and user hasn't subscribed
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py - Added push notification endpoints and send_push_notification function
context_for_next_testing_agent: Push notifications are now available. The 'Enable Desktop Alerts' button appears in the notification dropdown when the browser supports push and the user hasn't subscribed. Subscriptions are stored in MongoDB 'push_subscriptions' collection. When any notification is created (ticket, document, etc.), it will also send a push notification to all subscribed devices for that user.
